messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following property management company tenant privacy notice for GDPR compliance:

"PropManage collects your name, contact details, employment information, credit history, and bank details to assess tenancy applications and manage your tenancy. We process this under contract and legal obligation. References from employers and previous landlords are obtained with your consent. Tenancy records are retained for 6 years after tenancy end. We use a property management software provider (Fixflo, UK-based) under a DPA. You have the right to access and correct your data. Contact privacy@propmanage.com."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Contract (Art. 6(1)(b)) for tenancy management and legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c)) for regulatory compliance. Consent (Art. 6(1)(a)) for references. Multiple bases correctly applied.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Credit history and employment data can reveal financial distress, disability, or other protected characteristics. No acknowledgement or safeguard stated.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Tenancy application assessment and tenancy management are clearly stated purposes.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is standard and proportionate for residential tenancy management.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] 6-year post-tenancy retention clearly stated.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Access (Art. 15) and rectification (Art. 16) mentioned. Rights to erasure, portability, restriction, and objection absent.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] Fixflo named as UK-based processor under a DPA. Employer and landlord reference providers also disclosed.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PARTIAL] Fixflo is UK-based. If credit referencing involves non-UK agencies, transfers are not addressed.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not addressed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 9 (credit and employment data sensitivity not addressed), Art. 15-21 (erasure, portability, restriction, objection rights absent), Art. 37 (DPO not addressed), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Acknowledge that credit and employment data may reveal protected characteristics and state appropriate safeguards
- Add erasure, portability, restriction, and objection rights
- Confirm DPO requirement and disclose contact if applicable
- Add breach notification commitment
- Address international transfer implications if credit referencing involves non-UK agencies
